sibling_files: บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_02_หน้า39_bathroom_plan.json
warnings: GRID CONFIRMATION: the printed dimension chain on this page (horizontal 3.20+3.20+3.20=9.60 under circled grid labels 1/2/3/4; vertical 4.00+2.00+3.60+1.80+3.15=14.55 beside circled letters F/E/D/C/B/A) exactly matches this house's grid master (หน้า00_gridline.json): grid1(0.0)-grid2(3.2)-grid3(6.4)-grid4(9.6), and F(0.0)-E(4.0)-D(6.0)-C(9.6)-B(11.4)-A(14.55). No dummy/prime grid lines were needed on this page.

This house has TWO septic tanks and appears to have two 'ห้องน้ำ 1' rooms with the same label (one near grid1-2, one near grid3-4) -- transcribed as printed; the duplicate room name is not a transcription error, both bathrooms are labeled identically on the sheet.

This page also contains a smaller inset detail 'แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลห้องน้ำ' (1:50, ท่อประปาเดินใต้พื้น), captured in the sibling file, plus the septic tank / grease trap spec notes reproduced above in equipment_specs (text block matches the standard boilerplate also seen on house #1's equivalent page).

Exact routing of individual small-diameter branch CW pipes into each room, and the precise identity/count of every perimeter X-in-square catch-basin/access symbol, is difficult to trace precisely at this resolution; positions and the count are best-effort/approximate, not measured coordinates -- flagged for zoom-review.

Room level labels (+0.60, +0.50, +0.20, ±0.00) read with high confidence; exact wall-to-wall room boundaries in the middle of the plan (ทานอาหาร/พักผ่อน/นอน3 area) are partially obscured by overlapping pipe-routing lines.
